title: X algorithm hacks (from the actual code)

slides.headline: X open-sourced their algorithm
slides.body: Here's what I found in the code
slides.visual_note: GitHub code snippet background, X logo

slides.headline: Hack 1: Images get 2x boost
slides.body: The code literally doubles the score for tweets with images. Video gets 1.5x. Text-only gets 1x.

slides.headline: Hack 2: Replies matter more than likes
slides.body: Reply weight: 1.0. Like weight: 0.5. Retweet weight: 0.3. The algorithm wants conversations, not applause.

slides.headline: Hack 3: Link penalty is real
slides.body: External links get -0.5 score. Put links in comments, not the main tweet. Or use reply-to-self pattern.

slides.headline: Hack 4: First hour is everything
slides.body: Time decay is aggressive. Tweet performance in first 60 min determines total reach. Front-load engagement.

slides.headline: Hack 5: Thread boost
slides.body: Threads get algorithmic boost. Each reply in your own thread compounds. 4-7 tweets is the sweet spot.

slides.headline: Hack 6: Negative signals tank you
slides.body: Mutes, unfollows, and 'not interested' clicks are tracked. One viral tweet to wrong audience can hurt your account.

slides.headline: Hack 7: Blue check still matters
slides.body: Verified accounts get ranking boost. $8/month = more reach. The math works out for most creators.

slides.headline: The summary
slides.body: Post images. Ask questions. Avoid links in main tweet. Thread your best content. Engage in first hour.

cta_slide.headline: I test these hacks every week
